records.query: site:viennatools.github.io/ViennaPS ray tracing raysPerPoint sourcePower stickingProbability MultiParticleProcess
records.applicability: Directly defines the implemented model inputs, not equipment settings.
records.interpretation: Sticking is bounded from 0 to 1. Source exponent controls a power-cosine direction distribution. Example exponents are not a recommended range.
records.next_action: Use the mathematical sticking bound only. Range-find the practical interval on the target geometry.

records.query: site:viennatools.github.io/ViennaPS ray tracing raysPerPoint sourcePower stickingProbability MultiParticleProcess
records.geometry: Documentation example; target geometry not specified
records.interpretation: The documented 500 rays per point and 0.25 time-step ratio are example values, not accuracy recommendations.
records.next_action: Use paired numerical ladders on representative TSV shapes before approving either value.

records.query: Bosch deep reactive ion etching model passivation cycle time source power primary paper ViennaPS
records.location: Model description and Bosch cycle formulation
records.geometry: Paper geometry and physical model differ from this normalized wrapper
records.interpretation: Physical fluxes, yields, and cycle times cannot be copied into the wrapper's normalized coefficients.
records.next_action: Range-find implemented Bosch factors in model space and preserve equipment mapping as unknown.

records.query: through silicon via conformal liner barrier seed deposition sticking transport primary paper aspect ratio
records.tool_or_chemistry: TSV liner, barrier, and seed integration
records.applicability: Supports the importance of deep conformality; chemistry and model differ.
records.interpretation: The paper does not map precursor or sputter settings to ViennaPS deposition amount or sticking.
records.next_action: Keep amount and sticking ranges exploratory until geometry-matched range finding and calibration exist.

records.query: through silicon via conformal liner barrier seed deposition sticking transport primary paper aspect ratio
records.applicability: Identifies equipment influences but not the repository surrogate.
records.interpretation: DC power, argon pressure, time, and temperature affect coverage. No quantitative mapping to all-angle fraction is established.
records.next_action: Treat equipment influences as qualitative and calibrate an arrival distribution before conversion.

records.query: through silicon via copper bottom-up fill suppressor transport primary paper NIST
records.tool_or_chemistry: Acid copper sulfate, polyoxamine suppressor, chloride
records.applicability: Supports bottom-up suppressor mechanism; geometry and chemistry differ.
records.interpretation: Suppressor/chloride chemistry and waveform can enable bottom-up filling. The paper does not calibrate the seven candidate model coefficients.
records.next_action: Keep access and kinetic factors separate and perform a matched 3D mechanism check before morphology tuning.

records.query: site:nist.gov TSV CMP copper dishing erosion selectivity primary paper
records.applicability: Search results discussed CMP measurements but did not establish transferable limits or model-rate ranges for this surrogate.
records.interpretation: No applicable range was found for removal duration or oxide/Cu rate ratio.
records.next_action: Retain unknown ranges. Search tool- and stack-specific CMP data after incoming plug geometry and loss criteria are defined.

records.query: site:viennatools.github.io/ViennaPS planarization isotropic process material rates documentation
records.applicability: Defines the surrogate removal law only.
records.interpretation: Material rates move interfaces normally and may be material-specific. Documentation examples are not CMP calibration.
records.next_action: Use the API domain for implementation checks and block recipe claims until material-loss calibration exists.

records.query: through silicon via copper chemical mechanical polishing dishing plug loss experimental study
records.location: Experimental; Impact of copper polishing pressure on via dishing
records.geometry: 10 micrometer diameter; 100 micrometer depth; 300 millimeter wafer
records.applicability: Shows equipment-level pressure/time effects for a different stack and geometry.
records.interpretation: Pressure schedule changes dishing, but those seconds and pressures cannot calibrate the height-dependent ViennaPS removal surrogate.
records.next_action: Use the paper to teach the physical controls only. Keep surrogate duration and rate-ratio ranges unknown until tool-specific calibration exists.
